The cold-air damming wedge has retreated from the region, and today is the first of several rather mild days. Skies are not going to be completely clear either today or tomorrow, and may be more cloudy than not at times, but this will not change the overall feel of the back half of this week. In fact, on Friday, anybody that isn't on top of a mountain should break into the 70s, in what should be the warmest day of the warm-up. Afternoon pop-up shower chances return to the forecast by the weekend, although don't expect the great big thunderheads of summer lore to come rumbling over the mountain. These will be lighter in nature. Enjoy the warmth!

Much, Much Milder

Last Updated Wed March 04 at 4:49PM by Alan Simmons

The cold-air damming wedge has retreated from the region, and today is the first of several rather mild days. Skies are not going to be completely clear either today or tomorrow, and may be more cloudy than not at times, but this will not change the overall feel of the back half of this week. In fact, on Friday, anybody that isn't on top of a mountain should break into the 70s, in what should be the warmest day of the warm-up. Afternoon pop-up shower chances return to the forecast by the weekend, although don't expect the great big thunderheads of summer lore to come rumbling over the mountain. These will be lighter in nature. Enjoy the warmth!

The first Monday Message for March is now available for our subscribers.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

Following a warm front moving north and clearing our region, temperatures become much milder today. Lows in the realm of the mid 40s tonight will be our coolest for a week, most likely, which is of course rather abnormal for early March in the mountains.

High pressure moves off the east coast and into the summerlike "Bermuda High" positioning, working in tandem with an upper-level ridge over our heads to deliver us sustained warmth for the forseeable future.

Scattered afternoon shower chances appear in the forecast by this weekend, and Sunday may see more widespread (but still fairly scattered) activity as a dying cold front approaches the area. As mentioned in the introduction, these won't be great big summertime toad-floating thunderstorms, but even in their lighter state a few rumbles of thunder aren't impossible.

After temperatures are slightly cooler on Sunday (by only a few degrees) we begin to warm back up again to kick off next week.

Those of you that have lived up here awhile know that usually, such a warm run this early in the year is bound to have chilly consequences at some point later in March. Expect no different this year.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
